issue.message: Don't include `android.R` here; use a fully qualified name for each usage instead
By convention, the custom view (`AvatarImageView`) and the declare-styleable (`LoadImageView`) should have the same name (various editor features rely on this convention)
By convention, the custom view (`CuteSpinner`) and the declare-styleable (`Spinner`) should have the same name (various editor features rely on this convention)
By convention, the custom view (`IndicatingListView`) and the declare-styleable (`Indicating`) should have the same name (various editor features rely on this convention)
By convention, the custom view (`IndicatingScrollView`) and the declare-styleable (`Indicating`) should have the same name (various editor features rely on this convention)
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Reflective access to mDisappearingChildren, which is not part of the public SDK and therefore likely to change in future Android releases
Field requires API level 29 (current min is 28): `android.provider.MediaStore#VOLUME_EXTERNAL_PRIMARY`
Field requires API level 30 (current min is 28): `android.security.keystore.KeyProperties#AUTH_BIOMETRIC_STRONG`
Do not use `@Nullable` in Kotlin; the nullability is already implied by the Kotlin type `UniFile?` ending with `?`
Call requires API level 30 (current min is 28): `android.security.keystore.KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder#setUserAuthenticationParameters`
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

Calling detach() and attach() in the same FragmentTransaction is a no-op, meaning it does not recreate the Fragment's view. If you would like the view to be recreated, separate these operations into separate transactions.

The attribute `android:allowBackup` is deprecated from Android 12 and higher and may be removed in future versions. Consider adding the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules` specifying an `@xml` resource which configures cloud backups and device transfers on Android 12 and higher.
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.
